title: Delta Financial Domain Model
description: Wire-facing shapes for the Delta financial domain model (D-001). Every object is closed (additionalProperties:false) and every field bounded, mirroring the Sentinel contracts' F-001 audit posture. Attribution fields (tenant_id/team_id/project_id/agent_id) reproduce the formats from Anoryx-Sentinel/contracts/events.schema.json so a Delta record joins a Sentinel event by identity. Money is integer minor units (cents); no monetary field is a float. This schema is Delta-owned reference for D-002+; the authoritative ledger DDL is deferred to D-003.

Money.description: Exact monetary amount: integer minor units (cents) + ISO-4217 currency. Never a float.

minor_units.description: Amount in integer cents. Bounded to the largest monetary value any wire contract carries (1e11).

Transaction.description: A balanced double-entry transaction. The balance/one-currency/one-tenant invariant is enforced by the Delta types and reconciliation checks, NOT expressible in JSON Schema alone.

CostCenter.description: cost_center_id is, by decision (Fork 1a), a Sentinel team_id.

Allocation.description: Total distributed across targets. Sum(targets)==total and one currency are enforced by the Delta types / reconciliation, not by JSON Schema.

BudgetConcept.description: Token/cost ceiling per period at a scope. Maps 1:1 to the LOCKED policy.schema.json BudgetLimitPolicy. limit_cost_cents is a stricter INTEGER here (Delta holds integer cents); it serializes into the wire `number` field unchanged. At least one of limit_tokens/limit_cost_cents is required (mirrors the wire anyOf).

UsageRecord.description: Per-request token/cost record mirroring events.schema.json UsageEvent. cost_estimate_cents is integer cents (quantized at ingest) and a client-side ESTIMATE, never an authoritative bill.

revenue_source_product.description: The source product that recorded the real monetization event (X-005). v1 accepts ONLY `rendly`; further sources can be added ADDITIVELY without breaking this contract. SERVER-RESOLVED from the authenticated caller (the holder of the dedicated per-source revenue-ingest HMAC secret) — it is NEVER read from the request body, exactly like the Orchestrator O-010/X-004 source_product discipline. It therefore does not appear on RevenueIngestRecord (the request shape); it appears only on RevenueRecord (the resolved, stored shape).

RevenueEventType.description: The monetization event class. v1 POSTS a ledger transaction for `subscription_granted` ONLY. `subscription_revoked` is accepted and durably recorded but does NOT reverse or void the granting transaction in v1 — automatic revocation/reversal posting is DEFERRED scope (a later X-005 follow-up). Nothing in this contract implies a revoke undoes a grant.

revenue_idempotency_key.description: Source-supplied dedup key, unique per source_product. A retried POST with the same (resolved source_product, idempotency_key) is an idempotent replay — recorded as such, never a second ledger transaction, never an error. Mirrors the Sentinel event_id -> ledger idempotency_key discipline used by the usage ingest path.

RevenueIngestRecord.description: X-005 wire request for POST /v1/ingest/revenue: ONE normalized 'a source product recorded a real monetization event' record. This is a GENUINE financial record (not metadata-only), but it is CLOSED and BOUNDED with the same discipline as UsageRecord and the Sentinel/Orchestrator event schemas. AUTH: this seam reuses Delta's EXISTING inbound-ingest trust model — a shared-secret HMAC signature over `{timestamp}.{body}` (`sha256=` prefix, ±300s replay window), identical in shape to the Orchestrator->Delta usage seam (ADR-0004 §3.3), but keyed by a DEDICATED per-source revenue-ingest HMAC secret so the authenticated key IDENTIFIES the source product. `source_product` is SERVER-RESOLVED from that authenticated caller and MUST NOT be supplied in the body (it is deliberately absent from this shape). Money is INTEGER minor units only — there is no float field anywhere in this record. RESPONSE: 200 with a RevenueIngestResult (mirrors the usage ingest's synchronous accepted/idempotent-replay 200, NOT an async 202).

tier.description: The source product's monetization tier name (e.g. a Rendly premium tier). OPAQUE to Delta — recorded verbatim, never interpreted or mapped.

amount_cents.description: The monetization amount in INTEGER minor units (cents). Bounded to the same 1e11 ceiling as Money.minor_units. INTEGER ONLY — this is Delta; there is no float representation of money anywhere in this contract.

currency.description: OPTIONAL ISO-4217 currency for the recorded amount. When omitted the posting path applies Delta's DEFAULT_CURRENCY (same convention as the usage ingest path). Present so a source is never forced to assume a currency it does not use.

occurred_at.description: When the SOURCE product recorded this monetization event (ISO-8601; an explicit UTC offset is REQUIRED).

RevenueIngestResult.description: X-005 wire response for POST /v1/ingest/revenue (HTTP 200 for both a fresh accept and an idempotent replay, mirroring the usage ingest's 200 disposition). `applied` is true only when a ledger transaction was newly posted; for `subscription_revoked` (not posted in v1) and for an idempotent replay it is false, and `txn_id` names the transaction when one exists (null otherwise).

applied.description: True iff a NEW balanced ledger transaction was posted by this request.

idempotent_replay.description: True iff this (source_product, idempotency_key) was already recorded — a no-op replay, not an error.

txn_id.description: The posted transaction id when one exists; null for an accepted-but-not-posted event (e.g. v1 subscription_revoked).

RevenueRecord.description: The RESOLVED, stored X-005 monetization record: a RevenueIngestRecord after the server has resolved `source_product` from the authenticated caller and stamped `received_at`. Documented here as the Delta-owned domain shape (this file is the domain-model reference, not an endpoint list); a `subscription_granted` record joins its posted ledger Transaction by txn_id. NOTE: this seam is WRITE-ONLY — recorded revenue is read back through Delta's existing ledger/dashboard query surface (the ledger is the read authority), so no dedicated GET read-back endpoint is added (that would duplicate existing functionality).

received_at.description: When Delta durably recorded this event.

txn_id.description: The posted ledger transaction (subscription_granted); null when no transaction was posted (v1 subscription_revoked).
